summary: An arm blood pressure difference greater than 15 mmHg indicates suspected subclanian steal syndrome but does not correlate with the severity of stenosis. Severity is better predicted by peak systolic velocities measured on duplex ultrasound rather than arterial blood pressure disparity.

Severity is better predicted by peak systolic velocities measured on duplex ultrasound rather than arterial blood pressure disparity.. Answer choice: B
